what_it_does: Defines a set of React components for a Vi chat surface, including entity read status cards, missing-information prompts, approval prompts, message rendering, and a controlled composer that auto-scrolls messages.
observations: Uses global React destructuring for useEffect and useRef with local aliases.
Exposes all defined components on window rather than exporting modules.
Uses ViMark as a visual identity and activity indicator for Vi messages and read cards.
Entity icons are represented with emoji mapped from kind values.
ChatPanel auto-scrolls the message container when children changes.
Composer input is controlled by value and setValue props and sends on Enter unless Shift is held.
Several components mix class-based styling with inline styles.
standouts: Compact component set focused on conversation, entity-reading progress, prompts, and approvals.
Vi identity is expressed through ViMark, V/i typography, and connected-entity language.
ReadCard changes both label and icon state based on done.
conventions: CSS class names use a vi- prefix for chat-related elements.
Controlled inputs are used for both MissingPrompt and ChatPanel.
Conditional rendering handles submitted, done, user, and live-Vi states.
Inline event handlers are used throughout.
No ES module imports or exports are present.
concerns: Emoji entity icons may render inconsistently across platforms.
Inline styles duplicate presentation concerns inside component logic.
ChatPanel uses a single-line input while the hint mentions Shift + Enter for a new line.
Enter handling calls onSend without checking for an empty value in this file.
window globals make component availability dependent on script load order.
notes: This file is the local React surface for Vi chat interactions, status cards, prompts, approvals, and message composition.
questions: Where are ViMark and the referenced CSS classes defined?
Is the composer intended to support multiline input despite using an input element?
Should send actions be blocked when the controlled value is empty?
Are these window assignments part of the intended runtime module pattern?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose says this is a Vi conversation surface, and the file implements that through chat messages, a composer, and related conversational prompt/status components.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to present Vi as an assistant that reads connected entities, asks for missing details, requests approvals, and keeps the conversation moving through a simple controlled chat UI.
novelty: Uses small, task-specific conversation artifacts such as reading cards, missing-data prompts, and approval prompts alongside a chat composer.
contribution: Contributes a neutral set of Vi conversation UI substrates: entity-status rendering, missing-field collection, approval gating, user and Vi message presentation, and auto-scrolling controlled composition.
summary_for_embedding: React file defining Vi chat UI components for messages, entity read cards, missing prompts, approval prompts, and a controlled auto-scrolling composer.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a Vi assistant conversation flow around connected-entity reading, user replies, missing information, and approval steps.

symbols.does: Maps an entity kind string to an emoji icon and renders it in a small span.
symbols.description: EntityIcon provides a local visual vocabulary for entity types such as brandkit, master, gallery, analytics, files, pricing, and contact. Unknown kinds fall back to a document emoji.

symbols.does: Renders a status card showing whether an entity is being read or has been read.
symbols.description: ReadCard switches class state, label text, and leading icon based on the done prop. While not done, it shows the entity kind icon and an animated ViMark without its glyph; when done, it shows a checkmark and the word Read.

symbols.does: Renders a missing-information prompt with a controlled input before submission and a confirmed value after submission.
symbols.description: MissingPrompt displays a Missing label, the question text, and either an input plus Send button or a submitted confirmation row. It supports Enter-to-submit in the input and delegates value changes and submission through props.

symbols.does: Renders an approval request with text, subtext, and an Approve button.
symbols.description: ApproveCard is a compact approval checkpoint component. It places the main text and small supporting copy together, then calls onApprove when the user clicks Approve.

symbols.does: Renders either a user message or a Vi message with avatar, name, timestamp, and content.
symbols.description: Message branches on from === 'user' to show a user avatar SVG, You label, timestamp, and text wrapper. Other senders render with ViMark, a V/i name treatment, optional live animation when from is vi-live, and the children content directly.

symbols.does: Renders the chat container, message list, composer input, hint text, and send button while auto-scrolling to the bottom on content changes.
symbols.description: ChatPanel owns a ref to the message list and scrolls it to its scrollHeight whenever children changes. It uses controlled value and setValue props for the composer, disables input while disabled is true, and triggers onSend from Enter or the Send button.
